steps.title: Identify the plural subject
steps.content: The subject is “Multiple newspapers,” which is plural. An independent clause requires a verb form that agrees with this subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement for present tense
steps.content: The main verb must be conjugated (finite) to agree with the plural subject. For present tense plural subjects, the verb takes the base form “serve.” Choices B, C, and D offer non-finite forms—a past participle (having served), an infinitive (to serve), and a present participle (serving)—which cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. That matches choice A — A is correct.
